title: Create a Map
instruction: Create a `map[string]int` with alice→30, bob→25, carol→35. Then print alice's age.

title: Check if a Key Exists
instruction: Map lookups return (value, ok). Look up "go" in the langs map; if found print "found", otherwise "not found".
hint: if _, ok := langs["go"]; ok { fmt.Println("found") } else { fmt.Println("not found") }

title: Delete a Key
instruction: Use `delete(ages, "bob")` to remove "bob". Then check if "bob" exists and print "not found" if it's gone.
hint: delete(ages, "bob") — then if _, ok := ages["bob"]; !ok { fmt.Println("not found") }

title: Iterate over a Map
instruction: Use `for range` on the scores map to print each player's name and score on one line (e.g. "alice: 10").
hint: for name, score := range scores { fmt.Printf("%s: %d\n", name, score) }

title: Word Count
instruction: Split "the cat sat on the mat" by spaces, count each word in a map, then print the count for "the" (should be 2).
hint: for _, w := range strings.Split(sentence, " ") { counts[w]++ } — fmt.Println("the", counts["the"])
